q: The SI unit of angular velocity is _______.
In a _______ motion, the object just moves in a circle.
The direction of the tangential acceleration is _______ to the circular path.
The centripetal acceleration is in the _______ direction.
In the right-hand rule for the direction of angular velocity, the fingers point in the direction of _______.
The SI unit of linear displacement is _______.
If the angular speed is increasing, the directions of tangential velocity and tangential acceleration are _______.
A _______ body is a body that does not deform or change shape.
The ratio of the linear velocity to the angular velocity of a body in circular motion is the _______ of the circular path.
The direction of the angular velocity is _______ to the plane of rotation.
In a non-uniform circular motion, an object moves in a circular path with a _______ speed.
The SI unit of angular displacement is _______.
In a circular motion, the object just moves in a _______.
If the angular velocity changes at a constant rate, the angular acceleration is _______.
The centripetal acceleration is also known as _______ acceleration.
The motion of artificial satellites going around the earth at a constant height is an example of _______ motion.
When the angular velocity is _______, the angular acceleration and the angular velocity have the same direction.
A rigid body is a body that does not _______ or change shape.
The SI unit of tangential acceleration is _______.
In a _______ circular motion, there are tangential acceleration and centripetal acceleration.
A rigid body is a body that does not deform or change ___.
In rotational motion, all the particles constituting it undergo circular motion about a common ___.
If its angular velocity changes at a constant rate, then we can say that the angular acceleration is ___.
The equations for constant angular acceleration in rotational motion are analogous to equations for constant acceleration in ___ motion.
All particles of an object rotating around the axis of rotation move in a ___ path.
The instantaneous angular velocity $\omega$ of rotational object is the ___ for all the particles comprising the object.
The relation between arc length $s$ and angular displacement $\theta$, measured in radian, is ___.
Since the direction of linear velocity is tangent to the path, the linear velocity is also called ___ velocity.
The tangential acceleration of a rotating object is a measure of how fast the ___ changes.
The direction of the angular velocity vector is perpendicular to the plane of rotation, and along the ___.
The direction of angular velocity is usually specified by the ___.
When the angular velocity is increasing, the angular acceleration vector points in the ___ direction as the angular velocity.
When the angular velocity is decreasing, the angular acceleration vector points in the ___ to the angular velocity.
An object that moves in a circle at constant speed $v$ is said to experience ___ circular motion.
The change in direction of velocity constitutes an ___.
An object revolving in a circle is continuously accelerating, even when the ___ remains constant.
Acceleration $\vec{a}$ is in the same direction as $\Delta \vec{v}$, it also points toward the ___ of the circle.
For uniform circular motion, the centripetal acceleration vector points towards the centre of the circular path while the linear velocity vector is ___ to the path.
Hence, the centripetal acceleration and linear velocity are ___ to each other at every point in the path.
In nonuniform circular motion, an object is moving in a circular path with a ___ speed.
Thus, an object in nonuniform circular motion has a resultant acceleration that is the ___ of the centripetal and tangential accelerations.
Unlike tangential acceleration, centripetal acceleration is present in ___ uniform and nonuniform circular motion.
Note that the two acceleration vectors $\vec{a}_c$ and $\vec{a}_T$ are perpendicular to each other, with $\vec{a}_c$ in the ___ direction.
The magnitude of resultant acceleration $\vec{a}$ is, ___.
The direction of resultant acceleration $\vec{a}$ is, ___.
The tangential acceleration $a_T$ relates to angular acceleration $\alpha$ by the equation ___.
The relation between the magnitude of the linear velocity $v$ and the angular velocity $\omega$ of a particle is ___.
If the magnitude of the angular velocity of a rotating object changes, the magnitude of linear velocity will also change and the object will have a ___ acceleration.
During these time intervals, its angular velocity is changing therefore it has an ___ acceleration.
In a circular motion, the object just moves in a ___.
